title: Recommended assignments are operative unless rejected

content.principle: When the architect/operator recommends a target assignment and the human operator does not disagree or correct it, the recommendation is operative and must be recorded in the roster immediately.
content.rationale: Leaving accepted assignment recommendations as chat-only intent creates roster drift and increases operator coordination load. Narada's task-governed build-out requires mechanical roster state to track accepted assignment intent.
content.contract_patch: Add operational rules under Agent Roster as Assignment Source of Truth: recommended assignments are operative unless rejected; conversation state corrects stale roster state.
not_applicable_when: The human operator explicitly rejects or corrects the recommendation
The recommendation conflicts with task dependencies or active roster state that the operator has not acknowledged
The recommendation is framed as a hypothetical option rather than a target assignment
